detaileddescription.para: Maximum time (ms) to wait for a client to send complete headers after connecting. Enforced non-blockingly via the tick clock — not passed to any socket recv call.

briefdescription.para: Write a null-terminated string to the socket.

simplesect.para: true if all bytes were sent.

briefdescription.para: Parse the method string into enum.
Parse request line + headers from already-buffered data.

detaileddescription.para: This is a pure parsing function — no socket I/O. The caller guarantees that buf contains a complete header block ending with "\r\n\r\n".

parameterdescription.para: Client socket (stored in req for body reads).
Work buffer containing the raw request.
Number of valid bytes in buf.

briefdescription.para: Match a route against the request.
Drop the active client and reset to IDLE.

memberdef.argsstring: (SYN_Httpd *srv, uint16_t port, const SYN_HttpdRoute *routes, size_t route_count, uint8_t *work_buf, size_t work_buf_size)
memberdef.name: syn_httpd_init

briefdescription.para: Initialize and start the HTTP server.

detaileddescription.para: Creates a listening socket on the given port.

parameterdescription.para: Shared work buffer for request parsing + responses.

simplesect.para: SYN_OK on success, SYN_ERROR if bind/listen fails.

briefdescription.para: Handle the DISPATCHING phase — parse, route, respond, close.

detaileddescription.para: Called once when headers are fully buffered. Runs synchronously but all data is already in work_buf — no blocking socket reads needed for headers.

parameterdescription.para: Server instance with complete headers in work_buf.

detaileddescription.para: Accepts one connection, parses the request, dispatches to the matching route handler, and closes the connection. Use this if you prefer manual control over the server loop.

simplesect.para: SYN_OK if a request was handled, SYN_TIMEOUT if no client connected, SYN_ERROR on parse failure.

briefdescription.para: Stop the server and close the listener socket.
Begin the response with a status line.

detaileddescription.para: Must be called first. E.g., syn_httpd_status(resp, 200, "OK").

parameterdescription.para: HTTP response state to write to.

detaileddescription.para: Must be called after syn_httpd_status() and before syn_httpd_body().

parameterdescription.para: HTTP response state to write to.

briefdescription.para: Flush the header block — sends the blank line after headers.

detaileddescription.para: Automatically finalizes headers on first call. Can be called multiple times for streaming responses.

parameterdescription.para: HTTP response state to write to.
Pointer to binary data to transmit.

briefdescription.para: Send a string as the response body.

parameterdescription.para: HTTP response state to write to.

memberdef.argsstring: (const SYN_HttpdRequest *req, const SYN_HttpdResponse *resp, void *buf, size_t max_len)
memberdef.name: syn_httpd_read_body

simplesect.para: Bytes read, 0 at end of body, -1 on error.

briefdescription.para: Protothread task function for the HTTP server.

detaileddescription.para: Register this as a SYN_TaskFunc with user_data pointing to a SYN_Httpd instance. The task accepts a connection, handles one request, then yields back to the scheduler.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the cooperative protothread context.
Pointer to the scheduler task structure.

briefdescription.para: Minimal HTTP/1.1 server — fully non-blocking implementation.

detaileddescription.para: Designed around the cooperative scheduler: every call to syn_httpd_step() does a bounded amount of work and returns immediately. Socket I/O uses timeout=0 exclusively. Stale connections are timed out via the tick clock.
